pi_items.source: Direct query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Track in downloaded Chinook database from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database
FTS5 MATCH query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM catalog_fts WHERE catalog_fts MATCH 'Iron Maiden'
FTS5 MATCH query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM catalog_fts WHERE catalog_fts MATCH 'Iron Maiden'
FTS5 MATCH query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM catalog_fts WHERE catalog_fts MATCH 'blues rock' - Returns only 4 tracks with 'blues' in track name and 'rock' in genre or vice versa
FTS5 MATCH query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM catalog_fts WHERE catalog_fts MATCH 'blues rock'
FTS5 MATCH query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM catalog_fts WHERE catalog_fts MATCH 'Bach' - Returns 7 Bach compositions in database (all by Johann Sebastian Bach in Classical genre)
FTS5 MATCH query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM catalog_fts WHERE catalog_fts MATCH 'Bach'
Specified in setup_task.sh and is a valid sqlite3 database location in user home directory
DBeaver connection name - not verifiable without running the task
FTS5 table name specified in setup - not verifiable without running the task
Path specified in task.json - not verifiable without running the task

privileged_info_summary: The Chinook database is a real, public sample database sourced from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database containing 3503 tracks across 11 tables. When indexed into an FTS5 virtual table with columns from Track, Album, Artist, and Genre, FTS5 MATCH searches return the following results: 'Iron Maiden' returns 214 rows (within expected 200-220), but 'blues rock' returns only 4 rows (contradicts expected 35-60), and 'Bach' returns only 7 rows (contradicts expected 15-45). The Bach search limitation is due to only 7-8 Bach compositions being in the database. The 'blues rock' limitation reflects that no genre named 'blues rock' exists; results are tracks with 'blues' in any column from Rock genre or vice versa.
